finding: Phase-1 discovery (phase2-handoff-notes revision note, post-Step-9 gate simulation, 2026-06-10): a panel-suffixed token like 'FIG. 4B' is invisible to the figure-token regex \bfig(?:ure|\.|-)?\s*0*(\d+)\b because the trailing letter removes the word boundary after the digits. A draft using only 'FIG. 4B' would falsely orphan figure 4 (spurious FIGUSE-001 hard fail) and miss FIGREF resolution. This run worked around it by mandating the parseable form 'FIG. 4 (panel B)'. Latent for any patent with lettered sub-figures.
recommendation: Candidate gate-strengthening if it recurs: extend the figure-token regex to accept an optional panel-letter suffix, shipped with a test_gates.py case; until then keep the handoff-notes token-form workaround.

root_cause_artifact: thesis-architect/references/invention-summary-schema.md claim layer + phase2-handoff-notes trap list (claim-internal ordering semantics not pre-chewed)
finding: Claim-order misattribution: SS3 intro/narration (+ SS1 P2 inconsistency) presented the description's preferred wipe-first order as locked by claim 1, while claim 1 locks only the tail partial order (oil after first blade starts moving, second wipe after oil [8001]). Grounding hard-gate breach, invisible to all deterministic gates. Phase-1 traps covered the adjacent classes (claim-8 dependency, design-goal-vs-claim) but not claim-1 internal ordering semantics. [resolved in iter 2: order re-attributed to the description via [0021]/[0038]/[9015], claim lock restated precisely]
recommendation: Add a claim-internal-semantics trap class to the Phase-1 trap checklist: for each independent claim, state exactly which orderings/conditions the claim locks vs leaves open, so Compose narrates embodiment ordering as the description's preference, never as claim-mandated.

finding: Cover universalization (SS1 P1 / SS2 P1 / SS2 title): prose universalized 'the industry sprays its covers' although the fact-check-log (dlhbowles-spray-nozzle-patents) shows the nozzles can also spray a bare objective-lens surface; the verified invariant is non-contact cleaning, not cover-as-universal. [resolved in iter 2: rescoped to the supported non-contact invariant; SS2 title kept under explicit allowance]
recommendation: Tie external-fact prose scope to the fact-check-log entry's literal scope; assert a universal only when the log states one.

root_cause_artifact: essay-en-composer/references/section-blueprint.md (no paragraph word-length band for mobile rendering)
finding: Mobile wall-of-text: SS5 P3 124w, SS5 P5 120w, SS4 P1 115w exceed the words/12 > 8-line mobile heuristic. gate_structure STRUCT-001 counts sentences (max 8), not words, so 4-6-sentence walls of long sentences pass the warn-only gate unflagged. [resolved in iter 2: all three split at the recommended seams, confirmed by word arithmetic]
recommendation: Candidate gate-strengthening: add a word-count warn heuristic (~>110w per paragraph) to gate_structure; and/or add a mobile paragraph-length band to section-blueprint so Compose self-limits.

finding: Antithesis contrast-pair cluster (~6 instances, e.g. 'a blink, not an outage'): density reads as an AI-tell pattern; load-bearing pairs (two bold anchors + closing pair) legitimate, incidental ones not. [resolved in iter 2: incidental instance rephrased to 'For the camera, the event is a blink.', cluster reduced to ~5]
recommendation: If contrast-pair clustering recurs across essays, add judged density guidance to anti-ai-writing.md (voice fence respected: routes to anti-ai canon, not voice-profile).

root_cause_artifact: _shared/references/anti-ai-writing.md (emoji allowance conditioned on a canon pattern not visible inside the Phase-3 fence)
finding: Essay-end thinking-emoji attached to a blended closing (aphoristic landing + forward pointer): the allowance admits the emoji only for the closing-open-question pattern, but the voice-fenced editor cannot read voice-canon entries to verify the blended landing qualifies. Fence-design tension: the rule's qualifying condition lives outside the editor's fence. [carried to iter 2 as ED2-01]
recommendation: Make the emoji allowance fence-decidable: restate the qualifying condition mechanically in anti-ai-writing.md or deliverable-voice-rules.md (both fence-visible), or have Phase-2 thesis-trace carry a canon-conformance declaration the editor can trust. Never re-expose voice-profile or canon entries to Phase 3.

finding: Word-level doubling: 'renewed/renewable' repeated between the SS3 bold anchor and the next paragraph. [resolved in iter 2: 'renewable' cut, bold anchor owns the renewal beat alone]
recommendation: A bold anchor owns its beat; adjacent prose must not re-spend the same key word.

finding: Header caption vs SS3 P1 near-verbatim echo ('packaged around one exposed optic'). [resolved in iter 2: body varied to 'every part of it serving one exposed optic'; remaining conceptual refrain reads as deliberate]
recommendation: Vary caption-derived phrasing in body prose; keep refrains conceptual, not lexical.

finding: Embodiment-hedge compression cluster (SS2 P3, SS3 P5, SS3 P7): may/can hedges compressed in paraphrase; judged intentional restatement and accepted as-is (values byte-accurate to [0020]/[0021]/[0012], no derived arithmetic, hedges kept elsewhere). [closed in iter 2 re-verification]
recommendation: Acceptable while values stay byte-accurate and hedges survive nearby; watch for drift toward unhedged universals across essays.

finding: Bold emphasis added inside the claim-1 blockquote without an '(emphasis added)' notation; quote otherwise byte-matches q-8001-1. [resolved in iter 2: '(emphasis added)' appended to the attribution line]
recommendation: Citation format should require '(emphasis added)' whenever composer styling is introduced inside verbatim patent text.

finding: Inline source-pointer style inconsistency: one pointer written '(dlhBowles patents, in Sources)' against the bare-label style used elsewhere. [resolved in iter 2: all five inline pointers uniform bare-label style]
recommendation: Specify a single inline source-pointer style in x-articles-format-en.md so consistency does not depend on editor catch.

root_cause_artifact: _shared/references/anti-ai-writing.md (emoji allowance conditioned on a canon pattern not visible inside the Phase-3 fence)
finding: ED2-01 (carried from iter 1, same instance): essay-end thinking-emoji on a blended closing; the v2 closing run does carry an explicit open question two paragraphs up, so the rule is satisfied on its face, but the final paragraph lands on the aphorism and the voice-fenced review still cannot verify the cited canon entries (closing-open-question-tesla-yield-rate).
recommendation: SETI verify against the canon (closing-open-question entry + the aphoristic entry's essay-end marker): keep if the blended landing qualifies, otherwise drop the emoji; no text change needed either way.

root_cause_artifact: editorial-review pass-5 split recommendations vs deliverable-voice-rules 3-7 sentence band (two paragraph criteria not jointly specified)
finding: ED2-02 (new, revision-induced): the iter-1 mandated splits created two 2-sentence paragraphs in the closing run (44w B1 beat, 58w ships-or-not beat) and left SS3 P2 at 2 sentences, under the essay 3-7 sentence band; reads as deliberate cadence acceleration and benefits mobile rendering; accepted as-is. Fixing the pass-5 mobile-wall medium produced a pass-1 band underrun: the 8-line mobile ceiling and the 3-7 sentence band conflict at the margins.
recommendation: Accept as-is; if strict band compliance is wanted, merging re-approaches the 8-line mobile ceiling (~124w). The two paragraph-length criteria need joint specification so revision guidance cannot satisfy one by breaking the other.

finding: ED2-03 (residue of resolved iter-1 medium): cover universalization survives in two compressed positions, the SS2 title 'Spray the Cover, Not the Lens' (kept under iter-1's explicit allowance) and the closing aphorism pair 'The industry sprays its covers.'; body now carries the precise non-contact invariant twice, so the aphorisms read as deliberate compression backed by precise prose.
recommendation: Optional title/aphorism-level precision ('Spray It, Never Touch It'; 'The industry cleans at arm's length.'); otherwise accept as deliberate compression.

root_cause_artifact: essay-en-composer/references/section-blueprint.md (no paragraph word-length band for mobile rendering)
finding: ED2-04 (carried marginals): SS3 P8 107w (~8.9 lines; quote-integrated demotion applies) and SS4 P4 99w (~8.2 lines at the conservative 12 w/line divisor) sit at the mobile heuristic edge; neither is a wall in the iter-1 medium sense; no other paragraph exceeds the heuristic.
recommendation: Optional: split SS4 P4 after '...contaminants arriving mid-clean [0029].' (threat-close | reopen); SS3 P8 no action (quote-integrated).

finding: no findings (empty-pass marker: claim-repetition map rebuilt for v2, no new doubling, sentence-tightening sweep clean)
no findings (empty-pass marker: all 7 verbatim spans byte-matched against invention-summary Quotable spans and input/patent.md; no drift, no substantive change)
no findings (empty-pass marker: lead tension + anchor by sentence 3, closing returns to lead frame, format checks clean, pointer style uniform)
no findings (empty-pass marker: all 6 gates pass with zero findings - emdash, anchors, sources, banned, structure, figure_use)

root_cause_artifact: thesis-architect/references/invention-summary-schema.md claim layer + phase2-handoff-notes trap wording (trap 10 itself called the [8002]-[8005] limitations 'such floors')
finding: F1: claim 5's pinned Ms point ("martensite formation begins at about 50° C." [8005]) characterized as a 'floor' in §3 and §4 — a bound the claim does not state. Grounding hard-gate breach, invisible to all deterministic gates; sole driver of the round-1 revise-required and a full revision round. Root traces UPSTREAM to Phase 1's own phase2-handoff-notes trap 10, which grouped [8005] under 'such floors'. Same class as run-1's high (claim-1 embodiment order attributed to the claim): the invention-summary claim layer does not state per-claim what is locked vs open vs pinned. [resolved in v2: re-characterized as a pinned transformation point in both locations; verbatim quote untouched]
recommendation: Round-2 editor, carried for retro: 'phase2-handoff-notes trap 10 still calls the [8002]-[8005] limitations such floors — propose fixing the trap wording upstream so the next run does not re-inherit the drift.' Add a per-claim locked/open/pinned scope map to the Phase-1 invention-summary claim layer and restate it in trap form in the handoff notes.

finding: F2: §1 EOS scope overreach — prose stretched the logged fact eos-ms1-18ni300-aging ('typical applications include injection-molding tools and inserts') toward universality ('sold for exactly this kind of work'). Same class as run-1's dlhBowles cover universalization: external-fact prose scope exceeding the fact-check-log entry's literal scope. [resolved in v2: application scope named explicitly; 'among them' supported by the source's 'include']
recommendation: Run-1 recommendation recurs cross-essay: tie external-fact prose scope to the fact-check-log entry's literal scope; assert a universal only when the log states one.

root_cause_artifact: essay-en-composer/references/figure-rendering.md (per-panel condition attribution when narrating multi-panel figures)
finding: F3: FIG. 7 (panel B) protocol ambiguity — prose allowed panel A's 510/575 C protocol to be inferred onto B1, whose own treatment is different (10 h aging at 450 C, [0077]). [resolved in v2: B1's own conditions stated explicitly, byte-faithful to [0077]]
recommendation: When narrating one panel of a multi-panel figure, state that panel's own test conditions; never let an adjacent panel's protocol carry over by ambiguity.

finding: F4: unsourced 'this morning' — time-of-day detail beyond the sourced publication date. [resolved in v2: 'published today' (sourced date only); the doubled 'today' removed]
recommendation: Date-level facts only; no invented time-of-day color.

finding: F5: 'would hold a patent on the excluded middle' — ownership-of-the-quadrant read plus the 'patent' noun against the A1 pending posture (trap 1 otherwise honored; A1 sweep clean both rounds after fix). [resolved in v2: 'would hold a claim that sits on the excluded middle of die-shop metallurgy'; conditional grant framing kept]
recommendation: Conditional-grant prose must stay claim-shaped, never ownership-shaped, while the application is pending.

finding: F6: claim-21 scope qualifier dropped in paraphrase — 'at least a portion of the conformal cooling channel' compressed to full conformity. Same family as run-1's embodiment-hedge compression (qualifying language lost in paraphrase), but load-bearing this time (claim scope). [resolved in v2: partial-conformity qualifier restored: 'at least part of whose channel']
recommendation: Qualifiers on claim language are scope; paraphrase may compress style, never scope.

root_cause_artifact: _shared/references/anti-ai-writing.md (emoji allowance conditioned on a canon pattern not visible inside the Phase-3 fence)
finding: F7: 🤔 hosted on a declarative aphoristic landing — canon-sanctioned (circuit-contract) but the voice-fenced editor cannot read canon entries to verify, so the allowance is undecidable inside the fence. Escalation of run-1's carried low (same artifact, same gap) to medium. [resolved in v2: composer re-hosted the emoji on a genuine open question ('Can a die keep what the printer draws? 🤔') so anti-ai-writing's rule alone decides, no canon access needed; composer precedent note endorsed by the round-2 editor: prefer interrogative 🤔 hosts because declarative-aphorism + 🤔 is not verifiable under the Phase-3 fence]
recommendation: Make the emoji allowance fence-decidable in anti-ai-writing.md: require the hosting sentence itself be the open question (interrogative). Voice fence respected: routes to anti-ai canon, never voice-profile.

finding: F8: acceptable-repetition awareness flag — floors restated across lead fragment quote / §3 blockquote / FIG. 1 caption / closing synthesis; posture beat x3. Held: no action was required, and v2 added no fourth layer of either.
recommendation: Keep sanctioned repetition layers enumerated so revision does not add a new one unnoticed.

finding: F9: intensifier tic — 'exactly' x4. [resolved in v2: reduced to 2; §4 annealing-literature and §6 question instances kept]
recommendation: Sweep top-frequency intensifiers before handoff; keep only motif-bearing instances.

finding: F10: martensite unglossed at first use + FIG. 3 sweep jargon density, against the declared non-metallurgist audience (phase2-handoff-notes calibration: alloy vocabulary introduced through what it does to the part). [resolved in v2: functional gloss 'the hard structure steel snaps into when it cools fast'; sweep eased to three plain-language items, faithful to [0074]/FIG. 3 panels C-D as Phase 1 describes them; trap-10 'the document states the property and stops' retained]
recommendation: Gloss each load-bearing term functionally at first use; ease multi-clause technical sweeps to plain-language items without inventing mechanism.

root_cause_artifact: essay-en-composer/references/section-blueprint.md (no paragraph word-length band for mobile rendering)
finding: F11: §1p2 129w and §2p3 136w exceed the 8-line mobile ceiling; gate_structure STRUCT-001 counts sentences (max 8), not words, so both walls were gate-invisible — exact recurrence of run-1's medium (3 walls there, 2 here). 'Title'. Venue. URL" with checkable titles; authorless Energies entry kept deliberately — author list not reliably confirmable, and inventing or copying unverified names would be a fact introduction; the MDPI title's published em-dash normalized to a colon, disclosed in thesis-trace]

root_cause_artifact: editorial-review pass-5 split recommendations vs deliverable-voice-rules 3-7 sentence band (two paragraph criteria not jointly specified)
finding: Round 2: the F11 splits created paragraphs below the 3-7-sentence band (§1p3 at 2 sentences; two consecutive single-sentence §3 enumerations, 55w and 69w). Exact recurrence of run-1 ED2-02: fixing the pass-5 mobile-wall produced a pass-1 band underrun — the 8-line ceiling and the 3-7 band still conflict at the margins.
recommendation: The two paragraph-length criteria need joint specification so revision guidance cannot satisfy one by breaking the other.

finding: Round 2: with 'exactly' cured, next-frequency intensifier is 'own' x9; most instances carry the essay's central self-reference motif (deliberate repetition), two trimmable without loss. root_cause_artifact: essay-en-composer/references/section-blueprint.md (no paragraph word-length band for mobile rendering)
finding: Round 2: adjudication of the composer's three declared stands on retained 96w+ paragraphs — §1p1 114w (divisor-dependent; splitting would push the thesis out of lead paragraph 2), FIG. 3 paragraph 99w (grew by the editor's own F10 gloss; quote-integrated demotion applies), §6 gigacasting 111w (untouched per round-1's own instruction; in-band, one cohesive idea). finding: no findings (empty-pass marker: all 35 quoted patent spans re-verified byte-exact against input/patent.md; 48 anchor tokens in-index; claim-5 point characterization accurate in both §3 and §4; all external claims within fact-check-log tiers and wording; A1 posture sweep clean; goal-2 coverage sub-check clean — 4/4 core-mechanism layers, 7/7 selected figures engaged, asset-04 unreferenced)

no findings (empty-pass marker: all v2-altered paraphrases classified corrective or intentional restatements that increase/preserve source fidelity; claim-21 qualifier restored; source typos kept quoted and flagged; M.sub. artifacts inside quotes only, M2C/M3C/M2X in body voice)

Second consecutive run constrained by the lettered-panel regex blindspot: this patent's figures carry 16 lettered panel assets, and phase2-handoff-notes trap 3 again had to mandate the parseable form 'FIG. N (panel X)' and ban 'FIG. 2A'-style sole references so no figure would be falsely orphaned. The defect did not manifest (avoidance-by-convention worked: the round-2 edit-log confirms FIG tokens {1,2,3,5,6,7,8} all present and no letter-suffixed tokens), but the mitigation cost recurs per-run and one unconverted token would spuriously hard-fail FIGUSE-001.
recommendation: Run-1 set the bar 'candidate gate-strengthening if it recurs'. Determination: counted as recurrence of the mitigation COST, not of the failure. Extend FIG_RE/FIGREF_RE with an optional panel-letter suffix, shipped with test_gates.py cases (proposal on file); the prose '(panel X)' convention stays stylistically preferred.

root_cause_artifact: essay-en-composer/references/x-articles-format-en.md + strip-pipeline.md (no stated policy for em-dashes inside quoted bibliographic titles vs EMDASH-001)
finding: Accepted normalization on record (round-2 cross-check): the Energies paper's published title contains an em-dash; rendering it verbatim in # Sources would trip the zero-em-dash hard rule, so the entry normalized it to a colon, disclosed in thesis-trace, traceability preserved via venue/volume/URL. Improvised policy — no reference states how to handle source-verbatim em-dashes inside Sources titles.
recommendation: Editor: 'Retro may consider a reference note for quoted-title em-dash handling so future runs need not improvise.'

root_cause_artifact: _shared/scripts/gate_figure_use.py (selected-set harvesting scope: whole-file scan of figure-selection.md)
finding: gate_figure_use false-failed FIGUSE-001 on FIG. 4 and FIG. 5, which were explicitly NOT selected: the gate harvested figure numbers from the entire figure-selection.md, including the '## Not selected' table and the paired-relationship table that the handoff template schema requires. A schema-compliant Phase-1 artifact therefore guaranteed a spurious hard fail; both prior runs evaded it only because their selection files were free-form lists. Not a draft defect. [resolved mid-run: orchestrator scoped harvesting to the '## Selected figures' section with whole-text fallback for free-form files, added template-schema test_gates.py cases, regression pass, commit a980562]
recommendation: Gate code fix already applied and committed (do not re-propose). Residual gap: neither meta/fixtures/ case exercises the template-schema selection file, so the regression guard that gates proposal application cannot detect a re-regression of this class. Fixture-coverage proposal on file: 2026-06-12-figuse-selection-scope-fixture.

root_cause_artifact: thesis-architect/references/invention-summary-schema.md claim layer + phase2-handoff-notes trap list (claim-internal ordering semantics not pre-chewed)

Recurrence now 3/3 runs; promotion proposal on file: 2026-06-12-claim-scope-lock-map-promotion.

root_cause_artifact: thesis-architect/references/invention-summary-schema.md (claim layer carries no quote-anchor mechanism; [dddd] anchors exist only for spec paragraphs)
finding: EL-04: the claim-16 sentence (OR-alternative: actuator below / sensor above threshold) carries no [dddd] anchor — and cannot: claims have no paragraph numbers, so claim-text assertions are structurally unanchorable under the current anchor format. Round 1 correctly recommended 'No change required' for the draft; round 2 re-verified the sentence against patent.md claim 16. A draft-level non-defect exposing a pipeline-level gap: claim assertions are exempt from the anchor-verification machinery that protects every spec assertion.
recommendation: Retro candidate named by the editor both rounds: a Phase-1 claim-anchor mechanism (e.g. claim-level quote anchors c-NN-k in invention-summary.md's claim layer, mirroring q-dddd-k). Single occurrence as a logged finding; watch. Pairs naturally with the claim-scope lock map if promoted.

finding: EL-05: bold emphasis added inside the [0022] verbatim block quote without an 'emphasis added' declaration; quote body otherwise matched q-0022-1 byte-wise. recommendation: citation-format.md should state the emphasis-notation rule (any typographic emphasis inside a verbatim quote must be declared in the attribution line). Two records, both low/medium and fixed in one round; watch until a third.

finding: EL-06: the 'In some examples' embodiment qualifier was dropped when quoting q-0011-2, promoting example-embodiment language toward an unqualified statement.
